title: A New Recipe
summary: A narrator describes trying a new pasta recipe at home for the first time, from buying fresh ingredients at the market to sitting down and enjoying the finished dish.
body: Last weekend, I decided to try a new recipe I had found in a cooking magazine. It was a creamy tomato pasta with roasted vegetables. I went to the market on Saturday morning and bought everything I needed — fresh tomatoes, a courgette, a red pepper, some garlic, and a small pot of cream. Back home, I started by washing and chopping all the vegetables. I placed the courgette and pepper on a baking tray, drizzled them with olive oil, and put them in the oven at two hundred degrees. While the vegetables were roasting, I boiled a large pot of water and cooked the pasta until it was soft. In a separate pan, I fried the garlic in butter until it turned golden, then added the tomatoes and let them simmer for ten minutes. Next, I stirred in the cream and added the roasted vegetables from the oven. Finally, I mixed in the cooked pasta and served it with a little grated cheese on top. The whole dish took about forty minutes to prepare. When I tasted it, I was really pleased with the result. Cooking something from scratch always feels more satisfying than buying a ready meal.

annotations.exampleEn: She subscribes to a cooking magazine to find new recipes.
Roasted vegetables go well with grilled chicken.

annotations.meaningVi: tưới một ít dầu ô liu lên
annotations.exampleEn: Drizzle the salad with a little olive oil before serving.
Put the biscuits on a baking tray and place them in the oven.

annotations.phrase: boiled a large pot of water
annotations.meaningVi: đun sôi một nồi nước lớn
annotations.exampleEn: Boil a large pot of water before adding the pasta.
Fry the garlic in oil until it is golden and fragrant.

annotations.meaningVi: để chúng sôi nhẹ / liu riu
annotations.exampleEn: Add the sauce and let it simmer for fifteen minutes.
Stir in the cheese until it melts into the sauce.
Sprinkle some grated cheese over the pasta before serving.
She was pleased with the result of her first painting.

annotations.meaningVi: từ đầu / từ nguyên liệu thô (không dùng đồ có sẵn)
annotations.exampleEn: He baked the birthday cake from scratch using fresh ingredients.

annotations.meaningVi: bữa ăn đóng gói sẵn (chỉ cần hâm nóng)
annotations.exampleEn: On busy evenings, it's tempting to just heat up a ready meal.

grammarNotes.title: Sequence adverbs: first, while, next, finally
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để diễn tả các bước theo thứ tự trong khi nấu ăn hoặc làm bất kỳ việc gì, ta dùng các trạng từ chỉ trình tự. 'First' (đầu tiên), 'then' (sau đó), 'next' (tiếp theo), 'while' (trong khi), 'finally' (cuối cùng). Ví dụ: 'I started by washing the vegetables. While they were roasting, I boiled pasta. Finally, I mixed everything together.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use sequence adverbs to show the order of steps in a process. 'First / I started by' introduces the first step; 'while' shows two actions happening at the same time; 'then / next' introduce following steps; 'finally' introduces the last step. These are essential for describing recipes, instructions, or processes.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple vs. past continuous in recipes
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ đơn kể lại các bước hoàn thành: 'I washed the vegetables. I fried the garlic.' Thì quá khứ tiếp diễn mô tả hành động đang xảy ra khi một hành động khác chen vào: 'While the vegetables were roasting, I boiled the pasta.' Sự kết hợp hai thì này giúp câu chuyện trở nên sinh động và chính xác hơn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use Past Simple for completed steps: 'I washed the vegetables. I added the cream.' Use Past Continuous with 'while' to show two actions overlapping in time: 'While the vegetables were roasting, I cooked the pasta.' Combining both tenses makes a recipe narrative more natural and precise.

translationVi: Cuối tuần trước, tôi quyết định thử một công thức nấu ăn mới tôi tìm thấy trong một tạp chí nấu ăn. Đó là món mì ống sốt cà chua kem với rau củ nướng. Tôi đi chợ sáng thứ Bảy và mua tất cả những gì cần thiết — cà chua tươi, bí xanh, ớt đỏ, tỏi và một hộp nhỏ kem. Về nhà, tôi bắt đầu bằng cách rửa và cắt tất cả rau củ. Tôi đặt bí xanh và ớt lên khay nướng, tưới dầu ô liu lên và cho vào lò ở nhiệt độ hai trăm độ. Trong khi rau củ đang nướng, tôi đun sôi một nồi nước lớn và luộc mì cho đến khi mềm. Trong một chảo riêng, tôi phi tỏi trong bơ cho đến khi vàng, rồi thêm cà chua vào và để sôi nhẹ mười phút. Tiếp theo, tôi khuấy kem vào và thêm rau củ nướng từ lò vào. Cuối cùng, tôi trộn mì đã luộc vào và cho lên đĩa với một ít phô mai bào phủ lên trên. Toàn bộ món ăn mất khoảng bốn mươi phút để chuẩn bị. Khi tôi nếm thử, tôi thực sự hài lòng với kết quả. Tự nấu từ đầu luôn cảm thấy thoả mãn hơn là mua bữa ăn đóng gói sẵn.

questions.prompt: Where did the narrator find the recipe?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'I decided to try a new recipe I had found in a cooking magazine.'
questions.hint: It is a type of printed publication.

questions.prompt: What type of dish was the recipe for?
options: A creamy tomato pasta with roasted vegetables
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'It was a creamy tomato pasta with roasted vegetables.'
questions.hint: Both the sauce and the main ingredient are named.

questions.prompt: Where did the narrator go to buy the ingredients?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'I went to the market on Saturday morning.'
questions.hint: The narrator describes going somewhere specific on Saturday.

questions.prompt: Which of these was NOT mentioned as an ingredient?
questions.explanation: The narrator lists tomatoes, courgette, red pepper, garlic, and cream — mushrooms are not mentioned.
questions.hint: Check the list of ingredients the narrator bought at the market.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator put on the vegetables before putting them in the oven?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'drizzled them with olive oil.'
questions.hint: A liquid is described as being drizzled over the vegetables.

questions.prompt: What temperature was the oven set to?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'put them in the oven at two hundred degrees.'

questions.prompt: What did the narrator do while the vegetables were roasting?
options: Boiled water and cooked the pasta
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'While the vegetables were roasting, I boiled a large pot of water and cooked the pasta.'
questions.hint: The narrator did something with the pasta at the same time.

questions.prompt: What was the garlic fried in?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'I fried the garlic in butter.'
questions.hint: It is a dairy product used for cooking.

questions.prompt: How long did the tomatoes simmer?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'let them simmer for ten minutes.'
questions.hint: A specific cooking time is mentioned.

questions.prompt: What was added on top of the finished pasta dish?
questions.explanation: The narrator says the dish was served 'with a little grated cheese on top.'
questions.hint: A dairy topping is described at the very end of the recipe.

questions.prompt: How long did the whole dish take to prepare?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'The whole dish took about forty minutes to prepare.'
questions.hint: A total preparation time is given.

cloze.template: Last weekend, I decided to try a new {{b1}} I had found in a cooking magazine. It was a creamy tomato pasta with roasted vegetables. I went to the market on Saturday morning and bought everything I needed — fresh tomatoes, a courgette, a red pepper, some garlic, and a small pot of cream. Back home, I started by washing and {{b2}} all the vegetables. I placed the courgette and pepper on a baking tray, {{b3}} them with olive oil, and put them in the oven at two hundred degrees. While the vegetables were roasting, I {{b4}} a large pot of water and cooked the pasta until it was soft. In a separate pan, I fried the garlic in butter until it turned golden, then added the tomatoes and let them {{b5}} for ten minutes. Next, I stirred in the cream and added the roasted vegetables from the oven. Finally, I mixed in the cooked pasta and served it with a little {{b6}} cheese on top. The whole dish took about forty minutes to {{b7}}. When I tasted it, I was really {{b8}} with the result. Cooking something from {{b9}} always feels more satisfying than buying a {{b10}} meal.

blanks.explanation: 'recipe' — a recipe is a set of instructions for preparing a dish.
'chopping' — to chop means to cut food into small pieces.
'drizzled' — to drizzle means to pour a small amount of liquid gently over something.
'boiled' — to boil water means to heat it until it reaches one hundred degrees Celsius.
'simmer' — to simmer means to cook gently at a low heat just below boiling.
'grated' — grated cheese is cheese that has been shredded into small pieces.
'prepare' — to prepare a dish means to get all the ingredients ready and cook them.
'pleased' — to be pleased with something means to be satisfied or happy with it.
'scratch' — cooking from scratch means making a dish using raw ingredients, not pre-made products.
'ready' — a ready meal is a pre-cooked meal that only needs to be heated up.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says cooking from scratch feels more satisfying than buying a ready meal. Do you agree? What are the advantages and disadvantages of cooking at home versus buying pre-made food, and do you think the choice depends on lifestyle?
